priorities: Steady, predictable dividend and fixed‑income cash flow
Multi‑generational succession and legacy for grandchildren
redLines: No exposure to high‑beta or speculative asset classes
Absolute avoidance of speculative US software/technology stocks and AI‑driven mega‑caps
No aggressive strategy shifts or rebalancing into growth equities
preferredSectors: Consumer staples (e.g., Nestlé, Procter & Gamble, Walmart, Costco)
Insurance and financial services (e.g., Zurich Insurance)
avoidedSectors: Speculative US software and cloud/AI technology firms
Crypto and other non‑tangible speculative assets

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Long‑term, multi‑generational focus to preserve wealth for grandchildren
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Low immediate liquidity needs; occasional cash draws for family projects (e.g., home renovation) while keeping core equity intact
investmentBehavior.mandate: Conservative Defensive Global Value & Income Mandate

lifeEvents.description: CHF 150,000 withdrawn from cash accounts for a structural renovation of the holiday home in Flims.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity draw from cash; core defensive equity positions left untouched.

lifeEvents.description: Succession planning discussion; client requested a strict flag to block high‑beta speculative assets.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforced defensive bias and added explicit exclusion rules for speculative classes.

lifeEvents.description: Opened a minor savings account for the oldest grandson to instill traditional saving virtues.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Further emphasis on low‑risk, savings‑oriented products for the next generation.

lifeEvents.description: Account handover protocol initiated due to Thomas Keller’s early retirement.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Ensured continuity of the existing defensive allocation under a new relationship manager.

lifeEvents.description: Farewell lunch; client expressed trust in the bank’s institutional heritage to protect the conservative core portfolio.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Strengthened confidence in the bank’s stewardship; no portfolio change.

lifeEvents.description: Introductory call with new RM Sarah Meier; client reiterated expectation of a quiet, global defensive value approach.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Confirmed continuation of the existing mandate without aggressive shifts.

lifeEvents.description: Requested that all upcoming June bond coupons be swept to the family foundation.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Direct allocation of fixed‑income cash flow to philanthropic vehicle; no change to asset mix.

personalProfile.interests: Traditional saving virtues, family foundation, mountain retreats, tangible manufacturing
personalProfile.quirks: Formal and reserved demeanor; prefers "quiet, predictable, and boring" performance; avoids speculative trading apps
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: Mention of Swiss consumer staples, legacy foundations, industrial hardware (e.g., Siemens, ASML), and multi‑generational stewardship
personalProfile.avoidTopics: Speculative tech hype, competitor banks, high‑beta growth narratives

keyQuotes: "I want to sleep at night, not speculate on Silicon Valley cloud bubbles."
"These abstract software and AI firms are valued on pure hype. It feels like the dot‑com crash of 2000 all over again."
"We expect the same quiet, global defensive value approach that Thomas Keller provided. No aggressive strategy shifts."
